headline: Google Invests In Indonesian E-Commerce Giant, Joins Singapore Government, SoftBank, Alibaba
summary: Alphabet Inc’s (NASDAQ: GOOGL) Google unit has invested in Indonesian e-commerce giant Tokopedia, according to a Nikkei Asia report.

headline: Barron's Picks And Pans: Alibaba, Intel, Target, Visa And More
summary:
This weekend's Barron's cover offers a low-risk strategy for investors optimistic about an ecomomic recovery.
	Other featured articles discuss what a COVID-19 vaccine would mean for the stock market and how it could shake up the pharmaceutical industry.

headline: Last Week's Notable Insider Buys: Kraft, IBM, Vertex And More
summary:
Insider buying can be an encouraging signal for potential investors when markets face uncertainty.
	Insiders at some consumer products companies were adding to their stakes this past week.
	A few biotech companies saw insiders acquiring some shares as well.

headline: Uber's Self-Driving Unit Is For Sale: TechCrunch
summary: Uber Technologies Inc (NYSE: UBER) is looking to sell its self-driving unit, TechCrunch has reported.

headline: 3 Takeaways From The SpaceX Starlink Beta Test
summary: In the past couple of weeks, SpaceX has begun sending out invitations for users to beta test its Starlink low-earth orbit (LEO) internet service. On Thursday, DataTrek Research co-founder Jessica Rabe outlined three major takeaways from the Starlink beta test.

headline: Camping World CEO Lemonis Shows Off Lordstown EV Pickup In Twitter Videos
summary: Marcus Lemonis, CEO of Camping World Holdings Inc (NYSE: CWH), has shared three videos on Twitter of him interacting with and test driving a prototype from Lordstown Motors.

headline: Walmart Resumes Counting, Limiting Number of Customers in Stores Due to Spike in Coronavirus Cases; Retailers Placing Limits Again on Purchases of Toilet Paper
Benzinga's Bulls And Bears Of The Week: Apple, Disney, Tesla And More
summary:
Benzinga has examined the prospects for many investor favorite stocks over the past week. 
	The bullish calls this past week included top bank and aerospace stocks.
	Electric vehicle makers and hotels were featured among the week's bearish calls.

headline: 'Fast Money' Picks For November 16
summary: On CNBC's "Fast Money Final Trades," Steve Grasso recommended a long position in Sonos Inc (NASDAQ: SONO). He sees the stock 20% higher.
